title: triticum longisssimum subsp. sharonense
intro: Triticum longissimum subsp. sharonense, also known as Sharon wheat or Wild wheat, is a rare species of wild wheat that is native to the Mediterranean region. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it beneficial for soothing digestive issues and reducing inflammation. The health benefits of Triticum longissimum subsp. sharonense include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, improve blood circulation, and support immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and ground into a flour or tea, which can be used to make infusions or added to recipes for its nutritional and medicinal benefits.
